name: Diatam: Experiential
intuition: The gram presents the event as part of the subject’s life history. It answers the question of whether the event has occurred at least once, without asserting that a resulting state currently holds.
set_to_1_if: The clause contains explicit frequency adverbs indicating repetition or distinct instances (e.g., 'once', 'twice', 'often', 'ever', 'never', 'three times').
The gram is a negative Perfect (e.g., 'have not seen', 'has not shrunk'), which functions as a claim about the absence of the event in the subject's history.
The verb denotes perception or communication where the focus is on the occurrence of an experience at least once, rather than on a resulting state or transfer.
The verb denotes a bounded activity or process where the focus is on having undergone the experience, not on a persisting result.
set_to_0_if: The verb entails a change of state or location, and that resulting state holds at the reference time.
The verb entails a change of possession or social/legal status that remains in effect at the reference time.
The verb entails a transition into a new physical location, and the subject remains at that location at the reference time.
The clause contains a duration adverbial (e.g., 'for three days', 'since yesterday', 'all this time'), indicating a continuing situation rather than a completed life experience.
examples_positive: I have often visited that park. (Frequency adverbial -> 1)
She has visited that part three times. (Frequency adverbial -> 1)
Have you ever tried sushi? (Entry in subject's life history -> 1)
We have seen the Eiffel Tower. (Perception focusing on occurrence rather than result state -> 1)
examples_negative: He has been arrested. (Introduces result state [be in custody] -> 0)
The time has come. (Introduces current state of affairs -> 0)
I have broken the vase. (Introduces result state -> 0)
The guests have arrived. (Describes a physical change of location that persists at reference time -> 0)
They have traveled for three days. (Duration indicates an ongoing situation rather than a life-history entry -> 0)
We should have eaten. (Irrealis, unrealized situation -> 0)
